optionList.content: Sliding doors
Lever-style handles
Knob style door handles
Sliding door bolts with lock
Sliding doors

Sliding door bolts with lock

studentQuestionArray.content: A 59-year-old client has difficulty in opening the doors of his house due to bilateral weakness of the hands. What modification to the client’s home is the nurse most likely to recommend?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Sliding doors require more strength to open, and are not recommended for clients with weak hands.
Option 2:Lever-style handles for door and faucets are easy to operate, as they are easy for the client to grasp.
Option 3:Doorknobs are harder to grasp when compared to lever-style handles.
Option 4:Bolts give a small area for grip and sliding needs more effort when compared to lever style door operation.
Test Taking Tips: The type of change in the door would be determined by the grip factor and strength required to operate it.

A client who cannot understand accent
A client who cannot understand the language

studentQuestionArray.content: Which client is the best candidate for use of a closed captioned television (CCTV)?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:CCTV describes spoken language and nonspeech content including music or sounds in text form or symbols. This is required for deaf client to understand.
Option 2:Subtitles are transcript form of dialogues but do not have sound effects. It is for clients who can hear.
Option 3:If a client cannot understand language, subtitles can serve the purpose of understanding.
Option 4:CCTV can help to learn a language, but is intended for deaf clients.

optionList.content: Cooking food in microwave is easy and fast.
Cooking food in a glass dish in the microwave imparts very low metallic taste to the food.
Cooking food in the microwave using glass dishes utensils helps in easy cleaning and storage.
Cooking food in microwave will result in increasing the quality of nutrients.
Cooking food in microwave is easy and fast.

Cooking food in the microwave using glass dishes utensils helps in easy cleaning and storage.
Cooking food in microwave will result in increasing the quality of nutrients.

studentQuestionArray.content: A 78-year-old client diagnosed with cancer is on chemotherapy. The nurse serves food cooked in a glass dish in the microwave. The nurse also uses glass utensils to serve the food. What is the most accurate reason for this nursing action?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Though it is easy and quick, the main objective of cooking in microwave is to prevent distaste in food.
Option 2:Clients on chemotherapy have altered taste sensation. To minimize the metallic taste, the food should be cooked in microwave. Glass utensils are the best for cooking and serving.
Option 3:The main purpose of serving in glass dishes and with glass utensils is to minimize the metallic taste as metal utensils give a metallic taste.
Option 4:In any cooking process, the original nutritional value of food will decrease to some extent after exposure to heat.
Test Taking Tips: Chemotherapy may alter the sense of taste and smell and cause some clients to experience a metallic.

optionList.content: Cleaning the bulb syringe in warm, soapy water after use
Sucking the oral secretions by not inserting the bulb syringe into the throat
Suctioning the nasal cavities prior to suctioning the mouth with bulb syringe
Compressing the bulb syringe before it can be inserted into the nose
Cleaning the bulb syringe in warm, soapy water after use
Sucking the oral secretions by not inserting the bulb syringe into the throat
Suctioning the nasal cavities prior to suctioning the mouth with bulb syringe
Compressing the bulb syringe before it can be inserted into the nose

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse teaches the parent of a newborn baby about the proper use of a bulb syringe. Which action of the parent may cause complication in the baby?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:To remove the remaining oral secretion in the bulb syringe, prevent the growth of bacteria, and spread of infections, the bulb syringe should be cleaned in warm, soapy water after each use, every day.
Option 2:Touching the back of the throat and palate can trigger gag reflex. Therefore, it should be avoided.
Option 3:If there is respiratory obstruction, placing the bulb syringe in the nostril will activate an inspiratory gasp, which further draws the secretions into the respiratory tract. Nasal cleaning should be done after suctioning the oral cavity.
Option 4:Compression of bulb syringe before insertion creates negative pressure in it, and this negative pressure sucks the secretions. If it is sucked when it is in the mouth or nose, the gush of air from the bulb can push secretions further into the respiratory tract.
Test Taking Tips: Bulb syringes are used to clear the airway off the secretions.

studentQuestionArray.content: As part of post-operative orthopedic care, the nurse performed neurovascular assessment of a child who is subjected to skeletal traction. What is the upper limit for capillary refill time?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:If capillary time is less than three seconds, it is considered normal.
Option 2:If capillary time is more than three seconds, it is considered poor circulation.
Option 3:If capillary time is more than three seconds, it is considered poor circulation.
Option 4:If capillary time is more than three seconds, it is considered poor circulation. Poor perfusion will lead to ischemia damage in the distal tissue.

optionList.content: Put a pillow under the ankles
Elevate the head end of the bed
Administer morphine on PRN basis
Administer antibiotics to prevent infection
Put a pillow under the ankles
Elevate the head end of the bed
Administer morphine on PRN basis
Administer antibiotics to prevent infection

studentQuestionArray.content: A cast is applied to the left leg of a client with fracture of the left tibia. On examination, there is a swelling in the ankle. What should the nurse do first, if the client is at the risk of developing compartment syndrome?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Elevation of ankles relieves swelling by facilitating the flow of fluids into the lymphatic circulation. Prevention of compartment syndrome is a priority intervention.
Option 2:Elevation of the head end of the bed causes accumulation of inflammatory fluids in the dependent areas such as ankle.
Option 3:Morphine can give pain relief by acting centrally on the brain but it cannot reduce the swelling.
Option 4:For inflammation, antibiotics are not recommended.

studentQuestionArray.content: How often should the position of a child in a wheelchair be changed to prevent pressure ulcers?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:In order to avoid pressure ulcers, there should be change in the child’s position every 15 minutes.
Option 2:The minimum time for a change in position of a child confined to a wheelchair is 15 minutes.
Option 3:Sitting in a wheelchair for a long duration can cause pressure ulcers. To move the weight of the child off the bony prominences, the child’s position should be shifted every 15 minutes.
Option 4:The ideal time for changing the position of a child bound to a wheelchair is 15 minutes.

optionList.content: Gag reflex
Moro reflex
Glabellar reflex
Golgi tendon reflex
Gag reflex
Moro reflex
Glabellar reflex
Golgi tendon reflex

studentQuestionArray.content: What type of reflex should the nurse check in an unconscious client, before providing oral care?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:A gag reflex can prevents aspiration of fluids into lungs. So, the presence of gag reflex should be checked before brushing the teeth of an unconscious client.
Option 2:The Moro reflex is tested in an infant. A sudden stimulus such as sound causes the baby to startle and subsequently the baby will go into a protective fetal position.
Option 3:In positive Glabellar reflex, repetitive tapping on forehead would cause the eye to blink. It is positive in Parkinson’s disease.
Option 4:Contraction of a skeletal muscle causes relaxation of its antagonist muscle. If biceps contracts, triceps will relax in the arm simultaneously.
Test Taking Tips: During oral care, the unconscious client may aspirate.

optionList.content: Radiation
Conduction
Convection
Evaporation
Radiation
Conduction
Convection
Evaporation

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse advises the mother of a seven-day-old neonate to cover the baby with a stockinet cap to his dry head. What kind of heat loss can the stockinet cap prevent in the neonate?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:In radiation, heat transfer occurs between objects though they are not in touch.
Option 2:In radiation, heat transfer occurs between objects in direct contact.
Option 3:A current of cool air over the body parts causes heat loss. Head of the baby has relatively more surface area as compared to other body parts.
Option 4:In evaporation, heat is lost through the conversion of water into vapor. The baby’s head is dry, so heat loss can be very little in this situation.

optionList.content: Placing soft pads at the bony prominences
Withholding the iron supplementation
Applying ice over the reddened area to relieve the pain
Placing a smooth, hard board between the back of child and wheel chair
Placing soft pads at the bony prominences
Withholding the iron supplementation
Applying ice over the reddened area to relieve the pain
Placing a smooth, hard board between the back of child and wheel chair

studentQuestionArray.content: A child who uses a wheelchair for ambulation has a reddened area on the skin over his or her lower back. The child also takes iron supplements. What is the best nursing intervention to prevent decubitus ulcers in this child?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:If soft pads are placed between the bony prominences and wheel chair, the damage to the skin can be prevented by improving blood perfusion.
Option 2:Iron supplementation is recommended to improve blood formation and to prevent bed sores.
Option 3:Applying ice can decrease the blood flow by constricting the vasculature. It may aggravate the problem.
Option 4:Hard board causes more pressure over the bony prominences resulting in decreased blood flow and formation of pressure ulcers.

optionList.content: Perform the surgery as the girl is fit for it.
Advise the girl to avoid physical exercises.
Apply back bracing for 23 hours per day.
Administer transcutaneous electrical stimulation.
Perform the surgery as the girl is fit for it.
Advise the girl to avoid physical exercises.

Administer transcutaneous electrical stimulation.

studentQuestionArray.content: In a screening program, the primary health-care provider examines the x-ray report of a 12-year-old girl and notices scoliosis with a 28-degree curve. Her vital signs, ECG, and respiratory examinations are normal. What is an appropriate intervention?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Surgery is recommended if the scoliosis is over 40 degrees with respiratory complication in the girl.
Option 2:Exercises are recommended as they increase the strength in the back muscles, under the guidance of a physical therapist.
Option 3:Scoliosis between 10 and 40 degrees is considered mild. Back bracing for 23 hours/day stops the progression of scoliosis. The girl can remove it before taking a shower.
Option 4:Transcutaneous electrical stimulation is ineffective in improving the strength in the back muscles of the scoliosis clients.

studentQuestionArray.content: A child is diagnosed with a femur fracture. What should be the weight of the child to apply Bryant’s skin traction?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:If a child weighs less than 12 kg and has a fracture in femur, a Bryant’s skin traction can be applied. Here the pull is in only one direction. The counter traction is the weight of the child.
Option 2:For treating a child with a femur fracture by applying Bryant’s skin traction, the weight of the child should be less than 12 kg.
Option 3:In order to effectively treat a child with femur fracture using a Bryant’s skin traction, it is vital for the child to be less than 12 kg of weight.
Option 4:A Bryant’s skin traction is used to treat a child who weighs less than 12 kg.

studentQuestionArray.content: A 76-year-old client has pain in the right knee and complains of shortness of breath when walking. Which type of walking aid should the nurse recommend for this client?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:When the client has shortness of breath, the client may need to take rest intermittently by sitting or sleeping. Standing or walking with a crutch may aggravate shortness of breath.
Option 2:Using a walker with a seat may help the client to stop and sit in the seat and take some rest. This prevents aggravation and shortness of breath.
Option 3:Forearm crutches are recommended for clients with permanent limitations. These cannot give proper rest to the client when there is shortness of breath due to walking or standing.
Option 4:The client can stand with the support of a cane but it cannot prove to be helpful when the client has shortness of breath.

optionList.content: Down syndrome
Wilson syndrome
Turner's Syndrome
Sheehan's syndrome
Down syndrome
Wilson syndrome
Turner's Syndrome
Sheehan's syndrome

studentQuestionArray.content: The primary health-care provider prescribes lower limb braces to an eight-year-old girl diagnosed with Duchenne's muscular dystrophy (DMD). What other disorder could be associated with DMD in this client?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Trisomy of chromosome 21 is called Down syndrome. It is an autosomal disorder, whereas DMD is a sex-linked recessive disorder.
Option 2:Wilson syndrome is due to the congenital defect in Ceruloplasmin. It is not a chromosomal disorder.
Option 3:DMD is an X-linked recessive disorder. Only males are affected. Females are usually carriers, unless they have only one sex chromosome. In Turner's syndrome, the girl receives one X-gene from mother but does not receive another X-gene from father. If the X-gene from mother carries DMD, the girl will develop signs and symptoms of DMD.
Option 4:Post-partum pituitary necrosis is called Sheehan's syndrome.

optionList.content: It prevents nosocomial infections.
Hot water kills microorganisms in the bed pan.
Bacteria in feces would not grow in a warm bed pan.
A warm bed pan helps in the relaxation of the anal sphincter.
It prevents nosocomial infections.
Hot water kills microorganisms in the bed pan.
Bacteria in feces would not grow in a warm bed pan.
A warm bed pan helps in the relaxation of the anal sphincter.

studentQuestionArray.content: The nurse puts the metal bed pan under hot running water, dries it with a towel, and places the warm bed pan under the client’s buttocks. What is the most appropriate purpose of warming the bed pan in this situation?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:To prevent nosocomial infections, bed pans are sterilized by disinfectants or by autoclaving.
Option 2:Some bacteria and spores can survive even in hot water.
Option 3:Bacteria can grow in warm environments. If it is too hot, most of the bacteria cannot survive.
Option 4:If the bed pan is warm enough, the anal sphincters reflexively relax and it will help in passing stools freely.
Test Taking Tips: A cold surface may cause muscles to constrict.

optionList.content: Keep bedpan away from the client

Place a bed monitoring device to monitor the client
Unlock the wheels of the chair when transferring the client from bed
Keep bedpan away from the client
Lower the bed when giving care to the client
Place a bed monitoring device to monitor the client
Unlock the wheels of the chair when transferring the client from bed

studentQuestionArray.content: A 66-year-old postoperative client appears confused. What is the appropriate step to prevent the client from falls?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:The urinal and bedpan should be within the reach of the client, so that he need not get down from the bed.
Option 2:Lowering the bed prevents falls. The bed should be elevated for easy maneuver of the client.
Option 3:A bed monitoring device can be placed near the bed, which would alert the nurse when the client tries to get out of the bed.
Option 4:Unlocking the wheel while transferring the client can lead to movement in the chair, resulting in fall or injury.
Test Taking Tips: Many types of assistive devices are available to promote safety of clients who may be confused or disoriented.

optionList.content: Speak to the client with loud voice and repeat when required.
Give information to the interpreter, but speak directly to the client.
Give written information to interpreter and do not speak to the client.
Face the family and interpreter and speak to them, and allow the client to listen.
Speak to the client with loud voice and repeat when required.
Give information to the interpreter, but speak directly to the client.
Give written information to interpreter and do not speak to the client.
Face the family and interpreter and speak to them, and allow the client to listen.

studentQuestionArray.content: How would the nurse provide information about treatment options to a non-English speaking client who wears a hearing aid?
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Hearing aids amplify sounds 6 to 10 fold, so speaking loudly can cause the client discomfort.
Option 2:Though the client cannot understand English, the nurse should face and speak to the client because the client should know that the information is intended for him or her.
Option 3:The client has the basic right to know about his or her treatment options in the presence of the interpreter. The nurse can provide the written information later.
Option 4:The client should not feel ignored, so the nurse should face the client while speaking.

studentQuestionArray.content: A six-year-old child is diagnosed with Duchenne’s muscular dystrophy (DMD). The primary health-care provider prescribes braces on the leg. What should the nurse inform the child’s parents? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:For the prevention of constipation, stool softeners and laxatives are added in the treatment regimen of DMD.
Option 2:Swimming is recommended in DMD as it is a very low weight bearing activity.
Option 3:To prevent obesity in DMD children, a diet with low calories and high proteins is advised.
Option 4:Rarely, children diagnosed with DMD live beyond 20 years of age. Common cause of death is respiratory complications.
Option 5:In DMD, muscle fibers degenerate. Wasting and contractures of muscles lead to replacement with fat. Braces are applied to prevent contractures of muscles such as the calf muscles.
Test Taking Tips: Braces are assistive devices that can help manage certain musculoskeletal conditions.

studentQuestionArray.content: A 19-year-old client takes isotretinoin for acne and wears contact lenses for myopia. What should the nurse inform the client? Select all that apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Isotretinoin can cause a sudden decrease in night vision. Therefore, the client should avoid driving at nighttime.
Option 2:Isotretinoin does not change the color of tears.
Option 3:The skin may become dry when taking isotretinoin. Frequent washing of face aggravates dryness.
Option 4:The photosensitivity reaction is an adverse effect of isotretinoin. So, sun exposure should be avoided during treatment. When going out, the client should wear sun protective clothing.
Option 5:Isotretinoin can cause dryness in the eyes so the client should use eye lubricants.

studentQuestionArray.content: “The television programs shown on this hospital TV do not interest me,” says a client to the nurse. What instructions might the nurse give so as to distract the client from his or her dissatisfaction? Select All That Apply.
studentQuestionArray.textAnalysis:
Rationales
Option 1:Listening to radio provides distraction, so it can be encouraged.
Option 2:Music has proven healing effects and can distract the client, so it can be encouraged.
Option 3:Closed caption TV is intended for deaf clients.
Option 4:When the client does not want to watch television, he should be forced to do so.
Option 5:Books are alternatives to television for distraction. So, reading books should be encouraged.
